name: Pneumonia
overview: Pneumonia is a form of acute respiratory infection that affects the lungs, causing inflammation and fluid buildup. It is a leading cause of death in children worldwide, particularly in southern Asia and sub-Saharan Africa. Pneumonia can be caused by various infectious agents, including bacteria, viruses, and fungi.

causes.description: The most common cause of bacterial pneumonia in children.
The most common viral cause of pneumonia.
The second most common cause of bacterial pneumonia.

risk_factors.description: Increases risk due to inhalation of pollutants
Weakens immune system, making it harder to fight off infection
Increases risk due to weakened immune system

key_differentiators: presence of pus or fluid in the lungs

prevention.description: Prevents infection with certain bacteria and viruses
Reduces transmission of infectious agents
Helps to improve immune function

initial_questions.question: Has the patient experienced any difficulty breathing or rapid breathing?
Has the patient been exposed to anyone with a similar illness?
Has the patient recently traveled to an area with high rates of pneumonia?

refinement_questions.question: What is the patient's vaccination history, particularly with regards to pneumococcal and Hib vaccines?
refinement_questions.purpose: Identifies potential risk factors for pneumonia

refinement_questions.question: Has the patient been diagnosed with any underlying medical conditions, such as HIV or malnutrition?
refinement_questions.purpose: Identifies potential risk factors for pneumonia

refinement_questions.question: What is the patient's social history, including exposure to indoor air pollution and crowded living conditions?
refinement_questions.purpose: Identifies potential risk factors for pneumonia

red_flag_screening_questions.question: Is the patient experiencing severe difficulty breathing or chest pain?
Is the patient experiencing severe headache, confusion, or disorientation?

name: Asthma
overview: Asthma is a chronic lung disease that causes inflammation and muscle tightening around the airways, making it harder to breathe. Symptoms can include coughing, wheezing, shortness of breath, and chest tightness. With proper treatment, people with asthma can manage their symptoms and lead active lives.

causes.description: Family history of asthma increases the risk
Exposure to allergens, air pollution, and tobacco smoke can trigger asthma

distinguishing.name: Asthma attacks triggered by allergens or exercise

risk_factors.description: Increases the risk of developing asthma

risk_factors.factor: Exposure to allergens and air pollution
risk_factors.description: Triggers asthma symptoms

prevention.description: Reduces the risk of asthma attacks

initial_questions.question: Do you experience wheezing or coughing episodes, especially at night or during exercise?
Have you noticed any triggers that make your breathing worse, such as allergens or cold air?
Do you have a family history of asthma or other allergic conditions?

refinement_questions.question: Can you describe the pattern of your wheezing or coughing episodes?
refinement_questions.purpose: Differentiates asthma from other respiratory conditions

refinement_questions.question: Have you noticed any improvement in your symptoms when using an inhaler or other asthma medication?
Do you experience any symptoms outside of your lungs, such as skin rashes or digestive issues?

red_flag_screening_questions.question: Are you experiencing severe shortness of breath or difficulty breathing?
Do you have a history of severe asthma attacks or status asthmaticus?

name: Tuberculosis
overview: Tuberculosis is an infectious disease caused by bacteria that most often affects the lungs, spreading through the air when people with TB cough, sneeze, or spit. It is preventable and curable, but can be fatal without treatment. About a quarter of the global population is estimated to have been infected with TB bacteria.

causes.description: Mycobacterium tuberculosis is the bacteria that causes tuberculosis, typically affecting the lungs but can also affect other parts of the body.

symptoms.notes: Symptoms may be mild for many months, making it easy to spread TB to others without knowing it.

risk_factors.description: HIV infection increases the risk of developing TB by weakening the immune system
Poor nutrition increases the risk of developing TB by weakening the immune system
Smoking increases the risk of developing TB by damaging the lungs

key_differentiators: presence of TB bacteria in sputum or tissue samples

prevention.description: The BCG vaccine is given to babies or small children to prevent TB, especially in countries with high TB prevalence.
Good hygiene practices, such as covering the mouth and nose when coughing or sneezing, can help prevent the spread of TB.

initial_questions.question: Have you recently been in close contact with someone who has been diagnosed with TB?
Have you experienced any symptoms such as cough, chest pain, or weight loss for more than 2 weeks?
Have you been diagnosed with HIV or have a weakened immune system?

refinement_questions.question: Have you recently traveled to or lived in a country with high TB prevalence?
Have you been experiencing any symptoms such as coughing up blood or severe chest pain?
Have you been diagnosed with any other lung diseases, such as pneumonia or lung cancer?

red_flag_screening_questions.question: Are you experiencing any difficulty breathing or severe chest pain?
Have you been coughing up blood or experiencing severe hemoptysis?

name: Malaria
overview: Malaria is a life-threatening disease caused by a parasite and spread to humans by infected mosquitoes, mostly found in tropical countries. It is preventable and curable, but symptoms can range from mild to life-threatening. The disease does not spread from person to person, but can be transmitted through blood transfusion and contaminated needles.

causes.description: The Plasmodium parasite is transmitted through the bites of infected female Anopheles mosquitoes.

symptoms.notes: Symptoms may be mild for some people, especially those who have had a malaria infection before.

risk_factors.description: Increases risk of exposure to infected mosquitoes
Increases risk of exposure to infected mosquitoes

prevention.description: Reduces risk of exposure to infected mosquitoes
Reduces risk of infection
Reduces risk of infection

initial_questions.question: Have you recently travelled to a tropical or subtropical region?
initial_questions.purpose: Identifies potential exposure to infected mosquitoes

initial_questions.question: Have you experienced any cyclical fever patterns?
Have you been bitten by mosquitoes in the past two weeks?
initial_questions.purpose: Identifies potential exposure to infected mosquitoes

refinement_questions.question: What is the severity of your headache?
Have you experienced any confusion or disorientation?
Have you noticed any dark or bloody urine?

red_flag_screening_questions.question: Are you experiencing any difficulty breathing or chest pain?
Have you experienced any seizures or convulsions?

name: Dengue Fever
overview: Dengue fever is a viral infection caused by the dengue virus, transmitted to humans through the bite of infected mosquitoes. It is characterized by high fever, headache, and muscle and joint pains. In severe cases, it can lead to bleeding, low blood platelets, and blood plasma leakage, which can be life-threatening.

causes.description: The dengue virus is transmitted to humans through the bite of infected female mosquitoes, primarily the Aedes aegypti mosquito.

symptoms.notes: Some people may not exhibit any symptoms, while others may experience mild or severe symptoms.

risk_factors.description: Previous infection with dengue virus increases the risk of developing severe dengue
Urbanization, especially rapid and unplanned, can increase the risk of dengue transmission

key_differentiators: high fever, headache, and muscle and joint pains, with a sudden onset

prevention.description: Using insect repellents can help prevent mosquito bites and reduce the risk of dengue transmission
Wearing protective clothing, such as long-sleeved shirts and pants, can help prevent mosquito bites

initial_questions.question: Have you recently traveled to a tropical or subtropical area?
initial_questions.purpose: Identifies potential exposure to dengue virus

initial_questions.question: Have you been bitten by a mosquito in the past 10 days?
initial_questions.purpose: Identifies potential exposure to dengue virus

initial_questions.question: Do you have a history of previous dengue infection?
initial_questions.purpose: Identifies increased risk of severe dengue

refinement_questions.question: What is the severity of your abdominal pain?
refinement_questions.purpose: Differentiates from other diseases, such as Chikungunya

refinement_questions.question: Have you experienced any bleeding or bruising?
Do you have a rash or any skin lesions?
refinement_questions.purpose: Differentiates from other diseases, such as Zika virus

red_flag_screening_questions.question: Are you experiencing severe abdominal pain or vomiting?
Are you experiencing any bleeding or difficulty breathing?

name: Hepatitis A
overview: Hepatitis A is an inflammation of the liver caused by the hepatitis A virus, primarily spread through contaminated food or water, and can cause mild to severe symptoms. It is closely associated with unsafe water, inadequate sanitation, and poor personal hygiene. Hepatitis A does not cause chronic liver disease but can cause fulminant hepatitis, which is often fatal.

causes.description: The hepatitis A virus is the primary cause of the disease, and it is primarily spread through the fecal-oral route.

symptoms.notes: Symptoms are often mild in children, but can be severe in adults.

high_risk_groups: men who have sex with men

risk_factors.description: Increases the risk of hepatitis A transmission through contaminated food and water
Increases the risk of hepatitis A transmission through close contact with an infected person

key_differentiators: Detection of HAV-specific immunoglobulin G (IgM) antibodies in the blood

prevention.description: Prevents hepatitis A infection through the administration of inactivated hepatitis A vaccine
Reduces the risk of hepatitis A transmission through contaminated food and water

initial_questions.question: Have you recently traveled to an area with high endemicity of hepatitis A?
initial_questions.purpose: Identifies potential exposure to hepatitis A

initial_questions.question: Have you had close contact with someone who has been diagnosed with hepatitis A?
initial_questions.purpose: Identifies potential transmission through close contact

initial_questions.question: Do you have a history of injection drug use or men who have sex with men?
initial_questions.purpose: Identifies high-risk groups for hepatitis A transmission

refinement_questions.question: What is the severity of your jaundice?
refinement_questions.purpose: Differentiates hepatitis A from other causes of jaundice

refinement_questions.question: Have you experienced any episodes of diarrhea or vomiting?
refinement_questions.purpose: Identifies gastrointestinal symptoms associated with hepatitis A

refinement_questions.question: Have you recently eaten any undercooked or raw shellfish?
refinement_questions.purpose: Identifies potential source of hepatitis A transmission

red_flag_screening_questions.question: Are you experiencing severe abdominal pain or vomiting blood?
Are you experiencing signs of liver failure, such as confusion or bleeding?

name: Measles
overview: Measles is a highly contagious, serious airborne disease caused by a virus that can lead to severe complications and death. It spreads easily when an infected person breathes, coughs or sneezes. Symptoms include a high fever, cough, runny nose and a rash all over the body.

causes.description: a highly contagious virus

risk_factors.description: increases risk of infection
increases risk of complications

prevention.description: prevents infection

initial_questions.question: Have you recently been exposed to someone with measles?
Have you experienced a rash that started on your face and spread to other parts of your body?
Have you had a fever and cough for more than 3 days?

refinement_questions.question: Did your rash start with small white spots inside your cheeks?
Have you experienced any difficulty breathing or chest pain?
Have you been vaccinated against measles?

red_flag_screening_questions.question: Is there any difficulty breathing or chest pain?
Have you experienced any severe headache or confusion?

name: Scabies
overview: Scabies is a parasitic infestation caused by tiny mites that burrow into the skin and lay eggs, causing intense itching and a rash. It can lead to skin sores and serious complications like septicaemia, heart disease, and kidney problems. Scabies is contagious and spreads through skin-to-skin contact.

causes.description: The scabies mite burrows into the top layer of skin, where the adult female lays eggs, causing an allergic reaction and intense itching.

symptoms.notes: Symptoms may not appear until 4-6 weeks after infestation.

high_risk_groups: people living in crowded or poor sanitation conditions

risk_factors.description: Increases risk of scabies infestation due to closer living quarters and increased skin-to-skin contact.
Increases risk of severe scabies infestation, such as crusted scabies.

red_flags: severe itching that worsens at night

prevention.method: treating scabies as soon as possible
prevention.description: Prevents outbreaks and reduces risk of complications.

prevention.method: avoiding skin-to-skin contact with infested individuals
prevention.description: Reduces risk of transmission.

initial_questions.question: Have you noticed any intense itching, especially at night?
Have you had any skin-to-skin contact with someone who has been diagnosed with scabies?
Have you noticed any linear burrows on your skin?

refinement_questions.question: How long have you been experiencing symptoms?
refinement_questions.purpose: Differentiates scabies from other skin conditions

refinement_questions.question: Have you noticed any skin sores or crusts?
Have you been treated for scabies before?

red_flag_screening_questions.question: Are you experiencing severe itching that worsens at night?
Do you have a weakened immune system?
red_flag_screening_questions.purpose: Identifies potential increased risk of complications

name: Common Cold
overview: The common cold is a viral infection that affects the upper respiratory system, causing symptoms such as runny nose, sneezing, and coughing. It is one of the most common illnesses and can be caused by many different types of viruses. The common cold is usually mild and self-limiting, but it can be uncomfortable and disrupt daily activities.

causes.description: Rhinovirus is the most common cause of the common cold, accounting for 30-50% of cases.
Coronavirus is another common cause of the common cold, accounting for 10-20% of cases.

risk_factors.description: Poor hygiene increases the risk of transmission
Close contact with infected individuals increases the risk of transmission

prevention.description: Frequent hand washing can reduce the risk of transmission

prevention.method: avoiding close contact with infected individuals
prevention.description: Avoiding close contact with infected individuals can reduce the risk of transmission

initial_questions.question: Do you have a runny nose?
Have you been exposed to anyone with a cold?
Do you have a sore throat?

refinement_questions.question: How long have you had symptoms?
Do you have a fever?
Have you experienced any facial pain or pressure?

red_flag_screening_questions.question: Is there any difficulty breathing or chest pain?
Do you have a severe headache or stiff neck?

name: Influenza (Flu)
overview: Influenza, commonly known as the flu, is a contagious respiratory illness caused by influenza viruses. It can cause mild to severe illness, and at times can lead to hospitalization or even death. The flu is different from a cold, as it usually comes on suddenly and can cause severe fatigue, among other symptoms.

causes.description: One of the main types of influenza viruses causing seasonal epidemics almost every winter in the United States.
Another type of influenza virus that also causes seasonal epidemics.
Generally causes mild infections and is not thought to cause human flu epidemics.

distinguishing.name: Rapid onset of high fever, cough, and severe fatigue

symptoms.notes: Symptoms can vary by age and health status.

risk_factors.description: Conditions like HIV/AIDS, cancer, or taking immunosuppressive drugs increase the risk of severe flu.
Conditions like heart disease, lung disease, or diabetes can increase the risk of flu complications.

prevention.description: The most effective way to prevent the flu is by getting vaccinated each year.
Frequent hand washing, covering the mouth and nose when coughing or sneezing, and avoiding close contact with people who are sick can help prevent the spread of the flu.

initial_questions.question: Have you recently been exposed to someone with the flu?
Are you experiencing a sudden onset of high fever and severe fatigue?
initial_questions.purpose: Identifies key distinguishing symptoms of the flu

initial_questions.question: Have you had a cough that has worsened over the past few days?

refinement_questions.question: How long have you been experiencing your symptoms?
Have you had any difficulty breathing or chest pain?
Have you been vaccinated against the flu this season?

red_flag_screening_questions.question: Is there any difficulty breathing or chest pain?
Are you experiencing severe headache or confusion?

name: Sinusitis
overview: Sinusitis is an inflammation or infection of the sinus cavities, which are air-filled spaces in the skull. It can be caused by a viral, bacterial, or fungal infection, and can lead to symptoms such as nasal congestion, facial pain, and headache. If left untreated, sinusitis can lead to complications such as meningitis or brain abscess.

causes.description: Rhinovirus is a common cause of viral sinusitis
Streptococcus pneumoniae is a common cause of bacterial sinusitis

risk_factors.description: Allergies can increase the risk of developing sinusitis by causing inflammation and congestion in the nasal passages
Anatomical abnormalities, such as a deviated septum, can increase the risk of developing sinusitis by obstructing the flow of mucus and air through the nasal passages

prevention.description: Hand hygiene can help prevent the spread of viral and bacterial infections that can cause sinusitis
Avoiding allergens can help reduce the risk of developing sinusitis in people with allergies

initial_questions.question: Do you have a history of allergies or asthma?
initial_questions.purpose: Identifies potential underlying conditions that may contribute to sinusitis

initial_questions.question: Have you recently traveled or been exposed to anyone with a respiratory infection?
Do you have a history of anatomical abnormalities, such as a deviated septum?
initial_questions.purpose: Identifies potential anatomical factors that may contribute to sinusitis

refinement_questions.question: Can you describe the color and consistency of your nasal discharge?
refinement_questions.purpose: Differentiates between viral and bacterial sinusitis

refinement_questions.question: Have you experienced any facial pain or pressure, and if so, where is it located?
refinement_questions.purpose: Identifies the location and severity of facial pain

refinement_questions.question: Have you noticed any worsening of symptoms at night or when lying down?
refinement_questions.purpose: Identifies potential signs of chronic sinusitis

red_flag_screening_questions.question: Do you have a severe headache or confusion?
red_flag_screening_questions.purpose: Identifies potential signs of life-threatening complications

red_flag_screening_questions.question: Do you have difficulty breathing or experience chest pain?
red_flag_screening_questions.purpose: Identifies potential signs of respiratory distress

name: Tonsillitis
overview: Tonsillitis is an inflammation of the tonsils, which are two small glands located in the back of the throat. It can be caused by a viral or bacterial infection and is characterized by symptoms such as sore throat, fever, and swollen tonsils. If left untreated, tonsillitis can lead to complications such as abscesses or rheumatic fever.

causes.description: Adenovirus is a common cause of viral tonsillitis
Streptococcus pyogenes is a common cause of bacterial tonsillitis

risk_factors.description: A weakened immune system can increase the risk of developing tonsillitis

risk_factors.factor: Close contact with someone who has tonsillitis
risk_factors.description: Close contact with someone who has tonsillitis can increase the risk of developing the infection

key_differentiators: The presence of tonsillar exudate and swollen tonsils
red_flags: Severe sore throat that lasts for more than 2 days

prevention.description: Practicing good hygiene, such as washing hands frequently and avoiding close contact with someone who has tonsillitis, can help prevent the spread of the infection
Getting vaccinated against influenza can help prevent the spread of the infection

initial_questions.question: Do you have a sore throat that is severe and lasts for more than 2 days?
initial_questions.purpose: Identifies the severity of the sore throat

initial_questions.question: Have you been in close contact with someone who has tonsillitis?
Do you have a fever that is higher than 101.5°F?
initial_questions.purpose: Identifies the severity of the fever

refinement_questions.question: Do you have a white or yellow coating on your tonsils?
refinement_questions.purpose: Identifies the presence of tonsillar exudate

refinement_questions.question: Do you have difficulty swallowing or breathing?
refinement_questions.purpose: Identifies the severity of the symptoms

refinement_questions.question: Have you experienced any ear pain or hearing loss?

red_flag_screening_questions.question: Do you have difficulty breathing or chest pain?
red_flag_screening_questions.purpose: Identifies the need for emergency care

red_flag_screening_questions.question: Do you have a severe sore throat that is accompanied by a high fever and difficulty swallowing?
red_flag_screening_questions.purpose: Identifies the severity of the symptoms

name: Otitis Media
overview: Otitis media is an infection of the middle ear, which is the air-filled space behind the eardrum. It is a common condition that can cause pain, fever, and hearing loss. The infection can be caused by bacteria or viruses and can affect people of all ages, but it is most common in children.

causes.description: A type of bacteria that can cause a range of infections, including otitis media
A common virus that can cause respiratory infections, including otitis media

risk_factors.description: Young children are more susceptible to otitis media due to their underdeveloped Eustachian tubes
People with weakened immune systems are more susceptible to infections, including otitis media

prevention.description: Vaccines such as the pneumococcal conjugate vaccine can help prevent otitis media
Practicing good hygiene, such as washing hands regularly, can help prevent the spread of infections

initial_questions.question: Is the ear pain accompanied by a fever?
Is there any discharge from the ear?
Has the ear pain been persistent for more than 2 days?
initial_questions.purpose: Identifies possible need for medical attention

refinement_questions.question: Is the ear pain worse when lying down?
Has there been any recent upper respiratory infection?
refinement_questions.purpose: Identifies possible cause of otitis media

refinement_questions.question: Is the ear pain accompanied by a sensation of fullness in the ear?

red_flag_screening_questions.question: Is there any severe ear pain or fever over 102°F?
Is there any difficulty hearing or speaking?

name: Allergic Rhinitis
overview: Allergic Rhinitis is a common condition characterized by an overreaction of the immune system to particles in the air that are typically harmless to most people, such as pollen, dust mites, and pet dander. This overreaction leads to the release of chemical mediators, which cause blood vessels to swell, smooth muscle to contract, and mucous glands to increase secretion, resulting in symptoms like sneezing, runny nose, and nasal congestion. If left untreated, it can significantly impact quality of life and lead to complications like sinusitis and asthma.

causes.description: Substances that trigger allergic reactions, such as pollen, dust mites, mold, and pet dander.

high_risk_groups: individuals with a family history of allergies

risk_factors.description: Increases the likelihood of developing allergic rhinitis due to genetic predisposition

risk_factors.factor: Exposure to allergens at a young age
risk_factors.description: May sensitize individuals to specific allergens, leading to allergic rhinitis

prevention.description: Reducing exposure to known allergens can help prevent symptoms of allergic rhinitis
Allergy shots or sublingual immunotherapy can desensitize individuals to specific allergens

initial_questions.question: Do your symptoms worsen during specific times of the year or when exposed to certain environments?
initial_questions.purpose: Identifies potential seasonal or environmental triggers

initial_questions.question: Have you noticed any improvement in your symptoms when using allergy medications or avoiding certain substances?
initial_questions.purpose: Assesses response to potential treatments or allergen avoidance

initial_questions.question: Do you have a family history of allergies or asthma?
initial_questions.purpose: Evaluates genetic predisposition to allergic conditions

refinement_questions.question: Which specific allergens do you think might be triggering your symptoms?
Have you experienced any symptoms outside of the typical allergic rhinitis symptoms, such as fever or facial pain?
refinement_questions.purpose: Screens for potential complications or alternative diagnoses

refinement_questions.question: How have your symptoms impacted your daily activities and quality of life?
refinement_questions.purpose: Assesses the severity of the condition and potential need for intervention

red_flag_screening_questions.question: Are you experiencing severe difficulty breathing or shortness of breath?
red_flag_screening_questions.purpose: Identifies need for immediate medical attention

red_flag_screening_questions.question: Have you noticed any significant worsening of your symptoms over a short period, such as increased nasal congestion or facial pain?
red_flag_screening_questions.purpose: Screens for potential complications requiring urgent care

name: Gastroenteritis
overview: Gastroenteritis is an inflammation of the gastrointestinal tract, typically caused by a viral or bacterial infection, leading to symptoms such as diarrhea, vomiting, and abdominal pain. It can be acute or chronic and affects people of all ages. The infection can be spread through contaminated food or water, or through close contact with an infected person.

causes.description: A highly contagious virus that causes severe diarrhea and vomiting.
A type of bacteria that can cause food poisoning, leading to gastroenteritis.

modes: close contact with an infected person

symptoms.notes: Some people may experience a mild illness, while others may have a more severe infection.

risk_factors.description: Failing to wash hands regularly, especially after using the bathroom or before handling food, can increase the risk of infection.
Consuming food or water that has been contaminated with a virus or bacteria can increase the risk of infection.

prevention.description: Washing hands regularly, especially after using the bathroom or before handling food, can help prevent the spread of infection.
Handling and storing food safely can help prevent contamination and reduce the risk of infection.

initial_questions.question: Have you recently traveled to a foreign country or been exposed to someone with a similar illness?
Have you recently eaten undercooked or raw food, such as meat, poultry, or seafood?
Have you experienced any bloody stools or vomiting blood?

refinement_questions.question: How long have you been experiencing symptoms?
Have you experienced any fever or chills?
Have you recently taken any antibiotics or other medications that may have caused your symptoms?

red_flag_screening_questions.question: Are you experiencing severe abdominal pain or vomiting blood?
Are you experiencing signs of dehydration, such as excessive thirst or dark urine?

name: Irritable Bowel Syndrome
overview: Irritable Bowel Syndrome (IBS) is a chronic gastrointestinal disorder characterized by recurring abdominal pain, bloating, and changes in bowel movements. The exact cause of IBS is unknown, but it is believed to involve a combination of factors, including abnormal gut motility, hypersensitivity, and stress. IBS can significantly impact a person's quality of life, but it is not considered a life-threatening condition.

causes.description: The exact cause of IBS is unknown, but it is believed to involve a combination of factors, including abnormal gut motility, hypersensitivity, and stress.

distinguishing.name: Relief of abdominal pain with bowel movement

high_risk_groups: individuals with a family history of IBS

risk_factors.description: Stress can exacerbate IBS symptoms
Hormonal changes, such as those experienced during menstruation or menopause, can affect IBS symptoms

key_differentiators: Relief of abdominal pain with bowel movement

prevention.description: Avoiding trigger foods, increasing fiber intake, and staying hydrated can help manage IBS symptoms
Practicing stress-reducing techniques, such as meditation or yoga, can help alleviate IBS symptoms

initial_questions.question: Do you experience abdominal pain that is relieved by bowel movements?
initial_questions.purpose: Identifies a key differentiator of IBS

initial_questions.question: Have you noticed any changes in your bowel movements, such as diarrhea or constipation?
Do you experience bloating or discomfort after eating certain foods?

refinement_questions.question: Do you experience more symptoms during times of stress or anxiety?
refinement_questions.purpose: Assesses the impact of stress on IBS symptoms

refinement_questions.question: Have you noticed any correlation between your symptoms and your menstrual cycle?
Do you experience any other symptoms, such as nausea or vomiting, in addition to abdominal pain and changes in bowel movements?
refinement_questions.purpose: Assesses the presence of secondary symptoms

red_flag_screening_questions.question: Do you experience severe abdominal pain that is not relieved by bowel movements or medication?
red_flag_screening_questions.purpose: Identifies a potential red flag for IBS

red_flag_screening_questions.question: Have you noticed any blood in your stool or vomit?

name: Food Poisoning
overview: Food poisoning is an illness resulting from the consumption of contaminated or toxic food. It can be caused by a variety of pathogens, including bacteria, viruses, and parasites. Symptoms typically include nausea, vomiting, diarrhea, and abdominal cramps.

causes.description: A type of bacteria commonly found in contaminated food and water.
A highly contagious virus that can cause severe vomiting and diarrhea.
A parasite that can contaminate food and water, causing gastrointestinal symptoms.

modes: ingestion of contaminated food or water

distinguishing.name: sudden onset of symptoms after eating

symptoms.notes: Symptoms can vary depending on the type of pathogen and the individual's overall health.

risk_factors.factor: eating undercooked or raw meat, poultry, or seafood
risk_factors.description: Increases the risk of food poisoning by allowing pathogens to survive and multiply.

risk_factors.factor: not washing hands before handling food
risk_factors.description: Allows pathogens to spread and contaminate food.

key_differentiators: recent history of eating contaminated food
red_flags: severe vomiting that lasts more than 3 days

prevention.method: cooking food to the recommended internal temperature
prevention.description: Kills pathogens and prevents food poisoning.
Prevents the spread of pathogens and reduces the risk of food poisoning.

initial_questions.question: Have you recently eaten undercooked or raw meat, poultry, or seafood?
initial_questions.purpose: Identifies potential source of food poisoning

initial_questions.question: Have you noticed any blood in your stool or vomit?
initial_questions.purpose: Identifies potential complication of food poisoning

initial_questions.question: Have you traveled recently or eaten at a new restaurant?
initial_questions.purpose: Identifies potential source of food poisoning

refinement_questions.question: How long have you been experiencing symptoms?
refinement_questions.purpose: Differentiates between food poisoning and other gastrointestinal conditions

refinement_questions.question: Have you noticed any fever or chills?
refinement_questions.purpose: Identifies potential bacterial cause of food poisoning

refinement_questions.question: Have you eaten any high-risk foods, such as raw oysters or unpasteurized dairy products?
refinement_questions.purpose: Identifies potential source of food poisoning

red_flag_screening_questions.question: Are you experiencing severe vomiting that lasts more than 3 days?
red_flag_screening_questions.purpose: Identifies potential complication of food poisoning

red_flag_screening_questions.question: Do you have a weakened immune system or are you taking immunosuppressive medications?
red_flag_screening_questions.purpose: Identifies potential high-risk individual for complications of food poisoning

name: Chickenpox
overview: Chickenpox is a highly infectious illness caused by the varicella-zoster virus, characterized by a blister-like rash, itching, and fever. It is most commonly seen in children under the age of 10. Chickenpox is usually a mild illness, but it can be serious in certain groups, such as newborns, adults, and people with weakened immune systems.

causes.description: The varicella-zoster virus is the cause of chickenpox and later in life can reactivate to cause shingles.

distinguishing.name: Central distribution of rash with lesions in various stages

risk_factors.description: Increases the risk of severe illness
Increases the risk of contracting the illness

key_differentiators: Rash with lesions in various stages

long_term_effects: Increased risk of shingles later in life

prevention.description: Prevents or reduces the severity of chickenpox

initial_questions.question: Has the patient had a recent exposure to someone with chickenpox?
Is the rash itchy and does it have lesions in various stages?
Has the patient had a fever for the past few days?

refinement_questions.question: Did the rash start on the trunk and spread to other parts of the body?
Are there any signs of bacterial superinfection, such as increased redness or pus?
Has the patient experienced any breathing difficulties or severe headaches?

red_flag_screening_questions.question: Is the patient experiencing difficulty breathing or chest pain?
Is the patient experiencing severe headache or confusion?

name: Urinary Tract Infection
overview: A urinary tract infection (UTI) is an infection that occurs in any part of the urinary system, including the kidneys, ureters, bladder, and urethra. It is typically caused by bacteria and can cause symptoms such as burning during urination, frequent urination, and abdominal pain. If left untreated, UTIs can lead to more serious complications, such as kidney damage.

causes.description: E. coli is the most common cause of UTIs, accounting for approximately 80% of cases.

symptoms.notes: Some people may not experience any symptoms, especially in the early stages of the infection.

risk_factors.description: Women are more likely to develop UTIs due to their shorter urethra, which makes it easier for bacteria to enter the bladder.
Using a urinary catheter can increase the risk of developing a UTI.

key_differentiators: the presence of nitrites in the urine
the presence of leukocyte esterase in the urine

prevention.description: Helps to flush out bacteria from the urinary tract.
Helps to prevent the spread of bacteria.

initial_questions.question: Have you experienced any burning sensation while urinating?
initial_questions.purpose: Identifies dysuria, a common symptom of UTI

initial_questions.question: Have you noticed any strong-smelling urine?
initial_questions.purpose: Identifies a distinguishing symptom of UTI

initial_questions.question: Have you experienced any frequent urination?
initial_questions.purpose: Identifies a common symptom of UTI

refinement_questions.question: Have you recently used a urinary catheter?
refinement_questions.purpose: Identifies a risk factor for UTI

refinement_questions.question: Have you experienced any abdominal pain?
refinement_questions.purpose: Identifies a symptom that can distinguish UTI from other conditions

refinement_questions.question: Have you noticed any blood in your urine?
refinement_questions.purpose: Identifies a symptom that can distinguish UTI from other conditions

red_flag_screening_questions.question: Are you experiencing any severe abdominal pain?
red_flag_screening_questions.purpose: Identifies a red flag symptom that requires immediate medical attention

red_flag_screening_questions.question: Are you experiencing any vomiting?
red_flag_screening_questions.purpose: Identifies a red flag symptom that requires immediate medical attention

name: Migraine
overview: Migraine is a neurological condition characterized by recurrent episodes of severe headaches, often accompanied by sensitivity to light, sound, and nausea. It can significantly impact an individual's quality of life. Migraines are typically one-sided, pulsating, and can be exacerbated by physical activity.

causes.description: Migraines are believed to be caused by a combination of genetic and environmental factors, including hormonal changes, certain foods, and stress.

distinguishing.name: Aura symptoms, such as visual disturbances or tingling

symptoms.notes: Some individuals may experience a prodrome phase with symptoms such as mood changes, fatigue, and irritability before the onset of a migraine.

high_risk_groups: individuals with a family history of migraines

risk_factors.description: Fluctuations in estrogen levels can trigger migraines in some women.
Stress can trigger migraines in some individuals.

key_differentiators: The presence of aura symptoms, such as visual disturbances or tingling, can help differentiate migraines from other types of headaches.
red_flags: Sudden and severe headache, especially if accompanied by fever, stiff neck, or confusion.
typical_tests: Imaging studies, such as MRI or CT scans, to rule out other conditions.

prevention.method: Lifestyle modifications, such as maintaining a consistent sleep schedule and avoiding triggers.
prevention.description: Lifestyle modifications can help reduce the frequency and severity of migraines.

prevention.method: Medications, such as triptans and ergotamines.
prevention.description: Medications can help alleviate migraine symptoms and prevent future episodes.

initial_questions.question: Have you experienced any visual disturbances, such as flashing lights or zigzag patterns, before the onset of your headache?
Do you experience sensitivity to light and sound during your headaches?
Have you noticed any triggers that consistently precede your headaches, such as certain foods or stress?

refinement_questions.question: Can you describe the location and quality of your headache pain?
refinement_questions.purpose: Differentiate from other types of headaches

refinement_questions.question: Have you experienced any nausea or vomiting during your headaches?
Do you experience any weakness, numbness, or tingling during your headaches?

red_flag_screening_questions.question: Have you experienced a sudden and severe headache, especially if accompanied by fever, stiff neck, or confusion?
Have you experienced any recent head trauma or injury?
red_flag_screening_questions.purpose: Assess for potential causes of headache

name: Tension Headache
overview: Tension headache is a type of headache disorder characterized by a dull, aching sensation in the head and neck, often accompanied by muscle tension. It is the most common type of headache and can be caused by a variety of factors, including stress, poor posture, and muscle strain. Tension headaches can range from mild to severe and can be episodic or chronic.

causes.description: Muscle tension in the neck and scalp can lead to tension headaches
Stress can trigger tension headaches

high_risk_groups: individuals with a history of stress or anxiety

risk_factors.description: Poor posture can lead to muscle strain and tension, increasing the risk of tension headaches
Stress can trigger tension headaches

key_differentiators: The presence of band-like pressure around the head
red_flags: Headache with fever, stiff neck, or vomiting
typical_tests: Imaging tests (e.g. CT or MRI) to rule out other conditions

prevention.method: Stress management techniques (e.g. meditation, deep breathing)
prevention.description: Stress management techniques can help reduce the frequency and severity of tension headaches
Regular exercise can help reduce muscle tension and improve overall health

initial_questions.question: Do you experience a band-like pressure around your head?
initial_questions.purpose: Identifies a key differentiator of tension headache

initial_questions.question: Do you have a history of stress or anxiety?
initial_questions.purpose: Identifies a potential trigger for tension headaches

initial_questions.question: Do you have poor posture or engage in activities that strain your neck and scalp?
initial_questions.purpose: Identifies a potential risk factor for tension headaches

refinement_questions.question: How often do you experience headaches?
refinement_questions.purpose: Differentiates tension headache from other headache disorders

refinement_questions.question: Do you experience any visual or sensory symptoms with your headaches?
Do you experience any neck or shoulder pain with your headaches?
refinement_questions.purpose: Differentiates tension headache from other headache disorders

red_flag_screening_questions.question: Do you have a sudden and severe headache?
red_flag_screening_questions.purpose: Identifies a potential red flag for a more serious condition

red_flag_screening_questions.question: Do you have a headache with fever, stiff neck, or vomiting?
red_flag_screening_questions.purpose: Identifies a potential red flag for a more serious condition

name: Vertigo
overview: Vertigo is a type of dizziness characterized by the sensation that you or your surroundings are spinning or moving around. It is often caused by problems with the inner ear or vestibular system, but can also be related to neurological or psychological conditions. Vertigo can be debilitating and disrupt daily activities.
typical_duration: episodic, lasting from minutes to hours

causes.description: Benign paroxysmal positional vertigo (BPPV), labyrinthitis, or vestibular neuritis
Migraines, multiple sclerosis, or stroke
Certain antibiotics, antihistamines, or antidepressants

symptoms.notes: Can be accompanied by hearing loss or tinnitus

high_risk_groups: people with a history of ear problems
people with a history of neurological conditions

risk_factors.description: Increased risk with age, especially after 50
Increased risk if family members have a history of vertigo

key_differentiators: Associated with hearing loss or tinnitus
red_flags: Vertigo accompanied by weakness, numbness, or vision changes

prevention.description: Helps improve balance and reduce dizziness
Avoiding head movements or positions that trigger vertigo

initial_questions.question: Do you experience a spinning sensation when you move your head?
Have you recently experienced any ear problems or infections?
Do you have a history of migraines or neurological conditions?
initial_questions.purpose: Identifies potential underlying cause of vertigo

refinement_questions.question: Can you describe the sensation you experience when you have vertigo?
Do you experience any hearing loss or tinnitus when you have vertigo?
Have you noticed any triggers for your vertigo, such as certain head movements or positions?

red_flag_screening_questions.question: Do you experience any weakness, numbness, or vision changes when you have vertigo?
Have you experienced any sudden severe vertigo or loss of balance?

name: Eczema / Dermatitis
overview: Eczema, also known as dermatitis, is a condition characterized by itchy, inflamed skin. It can be caused by a variety of factors, including allergies, irritants, and genetic predisposition. Symptoms can range from mild to severe and can affect any part of the body.

causes.description: allergic reactions to substances such as pollen, dust mites, or certain foods
exposure to harsh soaps, detergents, or other chemicals

symptoms.notes: symptoms can vary depending on the type of eczema

high_risk_groups: individuals with a family history of eczema

risk_factors.description: increased risk of developing eczema if a family member has the condition
increased risk of developing eczema if an individual has other allergies

red_flags: severe itching that interferes with daily activities

prevention.description: reduces the risk of developing eczema
helps to reduce the risk of skin irritation

initial_questions.question: Have you experienced itching or redness on your skin?
Have you noticed any dryness or crusting on your skin?
Have you experienced any skin symptoms that have lasted for more than a few days?

refinement_questions.question: Have you noticed any lichenification on your skin?
refinement_questions.purpose: Differentiates eczema from other skin conditions

refinement_questions.question: Have you experienced any skin symptoms that are triggered by certain foods or substances?
Have you noticed any skin symptoms that are worse at certain times of the day or in certain environments?
refinement_questions.purpose: Identifies potential triggers or exacerbating factors

red_flag_screening_questions.question: Are you experiencing severe itching that is interfering with your daily activities?
red_flag_screening_questions.purpose: Identifies need for urgent medical attention

red_flag_screening_questions.question: Have you noticed any signs of skin infection, such as increased redness, swelling, or pus?
red_flag_screening_questions.purpose: Identifies need for urgent medical attention

name: Psoriasis
overview: Psoriasis is a chronic autoimmune skin condition characterized by thick, red, and scaly patches on the skin. It occurs when the immune system mistakenly attacks healthy skin cells, leading to an overproduction of skin cells and the formation of these patches. The exact cause of psoriasis is unknown, but it is believed to involve a combination of genetic, environmental, and immune system factors.

causes.description: overactive immune response leading to skin cell overproduction

distinguishing.name: Auspitz sign (bleeding points after scale removal)

symptoms.notes: symptoms can vary in severity and location

risk_factors.description: increased risk due to genetic predisposition
can trigger or exacerbate symptoms

long_term_effects: increased risk of other autoimmune diseases

prevention.description: reduces risk of symptom flare-ups
prevents symptom exacerbation

initial_questions.question: Have you noticed any thick, red, scaly patches on your skin?
Do you have a family history of psoriasis?
Have you experienced any recent stress or trauma?
initial_questions.purpose: Evaluates potential trigger for symptom flare-up

refinement_questions.question: Where did the skin patches first appear?
Do you experience any itching or burning sensations on the affected skin areas?
Have you noticed any joint pain or stiffness accompanying the skin symptoms?

red_flag_screening_questions.question: Are you experiencing widespread pustular psoriasis or severe skin pain?
Do you have difficulty moving or experiencing severe joint pain due to psoriatic arthritis?

name: Fungal Skin Infection
overview: Fungal skin infections are caused by fungi and can affect various parts of the body, leading to symptoms such as itching, redness, and skin lesions. These infections can be superficial or deep and are often contagious. Fungal skin infections can be treated with antifungal medications, but it's essential to seek medical attention if symptoms persist or worsen.

causes.description: A type of fungus that infects the skin, hair, and nails

risk_factors.description: increases risk of fungal skin infection due to moisture and warmth
creates an ideal environment for fungal growth

red_flags: severe itching that disrupts daily activities

prevention.method: keeping the skin clean and dry
prevention.description: reduces the risk of fungal skin infection by removing moisture and bacteria
helps to reduce sweating and moisture

initial_questions.question: Have you recently experienced any itching or redness on your skin?
initial_questions.purpose: Identifies potential fungal skin infection symptoms

initial_questions.question: Have you noticed any ring-shaped rashes on your skin?
initial_questions.purpose: Identifies distinguishing symptom of fungal skin infection

initial_questions.question: Have you recently been in contact with someone who has a fungal skin infection?

refinement_questions.question: Where did the rash or lesion first appear on your body?
Have you recently taken any antibiotics or immunosuppressive medications?
refinement_questions.purpose: Identifies potential risk factors for fungal skin infection

refinement_questions.question: Do you participate in any sports or activities that involve close contact with others?

red_flag_screening_questions.question: Are you experiencing severe itching or burning sensations on your skin?
red_flag_screening_questions.purpose: Identifies need for urgent medical attention

red_flag_screening_questions.question: Do you have a weakened immune system due to a medical condition or medication?
red_flag_screening_questions.purpose: Identifies potential risk for severe fungal skin infection

name: Lower Back Pain
overview: Lower back pain is a common condition that affects the muscles, bones, and nerves in the lower back, causing discomfort, stiffness, and limited mobility. It can be acute or chronic and is often caused by strain, injury, or poor posture. The pain can range from mild to severe and can radiate to other areas, such as the buttocks or legs.

causes.description: Overstretching or tearing of the muscles in the lower back
Bulging or rupture of the spinal discs
Prolonged periods of sitting or standing with poor spinal alignment

high_risk_groups: people with a history of back injuries
people who are overweight or obese

risk_factors.description: Prolonged periods of sitting or standing with poor spinal alignment can put strain on the lower back
Weak core and back muscles can contribute to lower back pain
Smoking can reduce blood flow to the discs and joints, leading to pain and stiffness

red_flags: Loss of bladder or bowel control

prevention.description: Strengthening core and back muscles can help prevent lower back pain
Reducing excess weight can take pressure off the lower back
Maintaining proper spinal alignment can reduce strain on the lower back

initial_questions.question: Have you recently lifted something heavy or engaged in strenuous activity?
initial_questions.purpose: Identifies potential cause of lower back pain

initial_questions.question: Do you have a history of back injuries or conditions?
initial_questions.purpose: Identifies potential underlying cause of lower back pain

initial_questions.question: Have you noticed any numbness or tingling in your legs or feet?

refinement_questions.question: Is the pain worse when you bend or twist?
Do you have any weakness or fatigue in your legs?
Have you noticed any changes in your bowel or bladder habits?

red_flag_screening_questions.question: Do you have any sudden severe pain or numbness in your legs?
Have you lost control of your bladder or bowels?

name: Osteoarthritis
overview: Osteoarthritis is a type of joint disorder that results from the breakdown of joint cartilage and underlying bone, leading to pain, stiffness, and limited mobility. It is the most common form of arthritis and can affect any joint, but it most often affects the hands, knees, hips, and spine. Osteoarthritis is a degenerative condition that worsens over time, but its progression can be slowed with proper treatment and lifestyle modifications.

causes.description: the breakdown of joint cartilage and underlying bone due to age, injury, or repetitive stress

symptoms.notes: symptoms can vary depending on the affected joint

high_risk_groups: individuals with a family history of osteoarthritis

risk_factors.description: the risk of osteoarthritis increases with age
excess weight puts additional stress on joints, increasing the risk of osteoarthritis

key_differentiators: joint pain and stiffness that worsens with activity and improves with rest
red_flags: sudden severe joint pain or swelling

prevention.description: reduces stress on joints and lowers the risk of osteoarthritis
strengthens muscles and improves joint mobility, reducing the risk of osteoarthritis

initial_questions.question: Do you experience joint pain or stiffness that worsens with activity and improves with rest?
Have you noticed any swelling or redness in your joints?
initial_questions.purpose: differentiates from other forms of arthritis

initial_questions.question: Do you have a family history of osteoarthritis?

refinement_questions.question: Which joints are affected by your symptoms?
refinement_questions.purpose: helps identify pattern of joint involvement

refinement_questions.question: Do you experience any grinding or crunching sensations in your joints?
Have you noticed any bony growths or nodules on your joints?

red_flag_screening_questions.question: Do you experience sudden severe joint pain or swelling?
Do you have a fever or chills in addition to your joint symptoms?
red_flag_screening_questions.purpose: identifies potential infection or other serious condition

name: COVID-19
overview: COVID-19 is a respiratory illness caused by the SARS-CoV-2 virus. It can range from mild to severe and can be life-threatening in some cases. The virus is highly contagious and can spread through the air when an infected person talks, coughs, or sneezes.

causes.description: a coronavirus that causes respiratory illness

symptoms.notes: some people may not show any symptoms

risk_factors.description: older adults are more likely to develop severe illness
people with certain medical conditions are more likely to develop severe illness

key_differentiators: travel history to an area with high COVID-19 transmission

prevention.description: getting vaccinated against COVID-19
staying at least 6 feet away from others

initial_questions.question: Have you recently traveled to an area with high COVID-19 transmission?
Have you been in close contact with someone who has COVID-19?
Are you experiencing any respiratory symptoms such as cough or shortness of breath?

refinement_questions.question: How long have you been experiencing symptoms?
refinement_questions.purpose: differentiates between COVID-19 and other illnesses

refinement_questions.question: Have you experienced any gastrointestinal symptoms such as diarrhea or nausea?
refinement_questions.purpose: differentiates between COVID-19 and other illnesses

refinement_questions.question: Have you been vaccinated against COVID-19?
refinement_questions.purpose: differentiates between COVID-19 and other illnesses

red_flag_screening_questions.question: Are you experiencing severe shortness of breath or difficulty breathing?
Are you experiencing chest pain or pressure?

name: Bronchitis
overview: Bronchitis is an inflammation of the lining of the bronchial tubes, which carry air to and from the lungs. It is usually caused by a viral or bacterial infection and can be acute or chronic. Symptoms include cough, mucus production, and shortness of breath.

causes.description: The most common cause of acute bronchitis
A common cause of bronchitis in young adults

risk_factors.description: Increases the risk of developing chronic bronchitis
Increases the risk of developing bronchitis

prevention.description: Getting vaccinated against the flu and pneumonia can help prevent bronchitis
Washing hands regularly and avoiding close contact with people who are sick can help prevent the spread of bronchitis

initial_questions.question: Do you have a cough?
How long have you had your cough?
Have you had a fever?

refinement_questions.question: Do you have wheezing or shortness of breath?
Have you been exposed to anyone with a respiratory illness?
Do you smoke or have you been exposed to secondhand smoke?
refinement_questions.purpose: Identifies risk factor for chronic bronchitis

red_flag_screening_questions.question: Do you have severe difficulty breathing or chest pain?
Have you had a recent injury or trauma to the chest?
red_flag_screening_questions.purpose: Identifies potential cause of severe symptoms

name: Strep Throat
overview: Strep throat is a bacterial infection of the throat and tonsils caused by group A Streptococcus. It is highly contagious and can cause severe symptoms. If left untreated, it can lead to complications such as kidney inflammation and rheumatic fever.

causes.description: a type of bacteria that can cause a range of infections

risk_factors.factor: close contact with an infected person
risk_factors.description: increases the risk of transmission

prevention.description: frequent hand washing and avoiding close contact with infected individuals

initial_questions.question: Do you have a sore throat?
Have you been in close contact with someone who has a sore throat?
Do you have a fever?

refinement_questions.question: How long have you had a sore throat?
refinement_questions.purpose: differentiates between acute and chronic conditions

refinement_questions.question: Do you have white patches on your tonsils?
Have you experienced any difficulty swallowing?

red_flag_screening_questions.question: Are you having trouble breathing?
Do you have a severe headache or stiff neck?

name: Acid Reflux / GERD
overview: Gastroesophageal reflux disease (GERD) is a chronic condition where stomach acid flows back into the esophagus, causing symptoms like heartburn and discomfort. It occurs when the lower esophageal sphincter (LES) does not close properly, allowing acid to flow back up. This can lead to complications like esophagitis and Barrett's esophagus if left untreated.

causes.description: abnormal relaxation of the lower esophageal sphincter

risk_factors.description: increased pressure on the stomach
relaxation of the lower esophageal sphincter

prevention.description: reduces pressure on the stomach

prevention.method: elevating the head of the bed
prevention.description: reduces reflux

initial_questions.question: Do you experience heartburn or regurgitation after eating?
Do you have difficulty swallowing?
Do you experience chest pain or discomfort?

refinement_questions.question: Does your heartburn worsen after lying down or bending over?
refinement_questions.purpose: differentiates from other causes of chest pain

refinement_questions.question: Do you experience relief from antacids or histamine-2 blockers?
refinement_questions.purpose: differentiates from other causes of dyspepsia

refinement_questions.question: Have you noticed any triggers for your symptoms, such as certain foods or drinks?

red_flag_screening_questions.question: Do you experience severe chest pain or difficulty breathing?
Do you have a history of bleeding or perforation in the gastrointestinal tract?

name: Appendicitis
overview: Appendicitis is a medical emergency where the appendix becomes inflamed, causing severe abdominal pain. If left untreated, the appendix can rupture, leading to peritonitis, a potentially life-threatening condition. Appendicitis requires prompt surgical intervention to remove the inflamed appendix.

causes.description: blockage of the appendix by feces, foreign objects, or other materials

risk_factors.description: having a family history of appendicitis increases the risk
a diet low in fiber may increase the risk

key_differentiators: tenderness in the lower right abdomen

prevention.description: eating a diet rich in fiber may help prevent appendicitis

initial_questions.question: Do you have severe abdominal pain?
Have you experienced nausea or vomiting?
Have you had a fever?

refinement_questions.question: Is your abdominal pain localized to the lower right abdomen?
Have you experienced rebound tenderness?
Have you had a recent change in bowel habits?

red_flag_screening_questions.question: Do you have severe abdominal pain that worsens over time?
Have you experienced vomiting blood or black tarry stools?

name: Peptic Ulcer
overview: Peptic ulcer disease is a condition where sores develop in the lining of the stomach or the first part of the small intestine. The most common symptoms are burning stomach pain and bleeding. If left untreated, peptic ulcers can lead to serious complications.

causes.description: infection with H. pylori bacteria

causes.pathogen_name: long-term use of nonsteroidal anti-inflammatory drugs (NSAIDs)
causes.description: irritation of the stomach lining

symptoms.notes: symptoms can vary in severity and frequency

high_risk_groups: people with a family history of peptic ulcers

risk_factors.factor: long-term use of nonsteroidal anti-inflammatory drugs (NSAIDs)
risk_factors.description: irritation of the stomach lining
reduces blood flow to the stomach lining

prevention.method: avoiding long-term use of nonsteroidal anti-inflammatory drugs (NSAIDs)
prevention.description: reduces irritation of the stomach lining
reduces stomach acid production

initial_questions.question: Do you have burning stomach pain that worsens with food?
Have you recently taken nonsteroidal anti-inflammatory drugs (NSAIDs)?
initial_questions.purpose: identifies potential cause of peptic ulcer

initial_questions.question: Do you have a family history of peptic ulcers?

refinement_questions.question: How long have you been experiencing stomach pain?
refinement_questions.purpose: differentiates between acute and chronic peptic ulcer

refinement_questions.question: Have you noticed any blood in your stool or vomit?

refinement_questions.question: Have you recently traveled to a country with high rates of H. pylori infection?
refinement_questions.purpose: identifies potential cause of peptic ulcer

red_flag_screening_questions.question: Are you experiencing severe abdominal pain that worsens with movement?
Are you vomiting blood or coffee ground-like material?

name: Typhoid
overview: Typhoid fever is a bacterial infection caused by Salmonella Typhi, typically spread through contaminated food and water. It can lead to serious complications if left untreated. Symptoms often develop within 6-30 days after exposure.

causes.description: a gram-negative bacterium

risk_factors.description: increases the risk of exposure to contaminated food and water
increases the risk of severe disease

prevention.description: getting vaccinated before traveling to endemic areas
practicing good hygiene, such as washing hands regularly

initial_questions.question: Have you recently traveled to a country with poor sanitation?
Have you experienced any abdominal pain or diarrhea?
Have you been vaccinated against typhoid fever?

refinement_questions.question: How long have you been experiencing fever?
refinement_questions.purpose: differentiates between typhoid and other infections

refinement_questions.question: Have you noticed any rash or skin lesions?
Have you experienced any severe abdominal pain or vomiting?

red_flag_screening_questions.question: Are you experiencing severe abdominal pain or vomiting?
Are you experiencing difficulty breathing or chest pain?

name: Type 2 Diabetes
overview: Type 2 diabetes is a chronic condition that affects the way the body processes blood sugar. It is characterized by high blood sugar levels, which can lead to a range of complications if left untreated. The condition is often associated with insulin resistance, where the body's cells become less responsive to insulin, a hormone that regulates blood sugar levels.

causes.description: a condition in which the body's cells become less responsive to insulin
a genetic tendency to develop insulin resistance and type 2 diabetes
excess body fat, particularly around the abdominal area

secondary.name: slow healing of cuts and wounds
tingling or numbness in the hands and feet

symptoms.notes: some people with type 2 diabetes may not experience any noticeable symptoms, especially in the early stages of the condition

high_risk_groups: individuals with a family history of type 2 diabetes
individuals with a history of gestational diabetes

risk_factors.description: excess body fat, particularly around the abdominal area, increases the risk of developing insulin resistance and type 2 diabetes
a sedentary lifestyle can increase the risk of developing insulin resistance and type 2 diabetes
a genetic tendency to develop insulin resistance and type 2 diabetes

prevention.description: through a combination of a healthy diet and regular physical activity
such as walking, cycling, or swimming, for at least 150 minutes per week
that is low in added sugars, saturated fats, and refined carbohydrates

initial_questions.question: Have you experienced any symptoms of high blood sugar, such as increased thirst or urination?
Have you been diagnosed with any other medical conditions, such as high blood pressure or high cholesterol?
Do you have a family history of type 2 diabetes?

refinement_questions.question: Have you noticed any changes in your vision, such as blurred vision or double vision?
refinement_questions.purpose: differentiates between type 2 diabetes and other conditions

refinement_questions.question: Have you experienced any numbness or tingling in your hands or feet?
refinement_questions.purpose: differentiates between type 2 diabetes and other conditions

refinement_questions.question: Have you noticed any changes in your appetite or weight?
refinement_questions.purpose: differentiates between type 2 diabetes and other conditions

red_flag_screening_questions.question: Are you experiencing any severe symptoms, such as chest pain or difficulty breathing?
Have you experienced any severe hyperglycemia or diabetic ketoacidosis?
